children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: For the purposes of items (cc) and (dd) of subclause (I) and except as provided in item (bb), a waiver of a potential conflict of interest, a waiver of an allocation limitation, or a similar activity, applicable to a transaction pursuant to the terms of an agreement governing an investment fund shall not be considered to constitute control of investment decisions of the fund or decisions relating to entities in which the fund is invested.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: bb Exception
children.text: The Committee may prescribe regulations providing for exceptions to item (aa) for extraordinary circumstances.

children.heading: Exception for air carriers
children.label: v Exception for air carriers
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B)(iii), the term “other investment” does not include an investment involving an air carrier, as defined in , that holds a certificate issued under section 41102 of that title.section 40102(a)(2) of title 49

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: vi Rule of construction
children.text: Any definition of “critical infrastructure” established under any provision of law other than this section shall not be determinative for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Country specification
children.label: E Country specification
children.text: The Committee shall prescribe regulations that further define the term “foreign person” for purposes of clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (B). In prescribing such regulations, the Committee shall specify criteria to limit the application of such clauses to the investments of certain categories of foreign persons. Such criteria shall take into consideration how a foreign person is connected to a foreign country or foreign government, and whether the connection may affect the national security of the United States.

children.heading: Transfers of certain assets pursuant to bankruptcy proceedings or other defaults
children.label: F Transfers of certain assets pursuant to bankruptcy proceedings or other defaults
children.text: The Committee shall prescribe regulations to clarify that the term “covered transaction” includes any transaction described in subparagraph (B) that arises pursuant to a bankruptcy proceeding or other form of default on debt.

children.heading: Critical infrastructure
children.label: 5 Critical infrastructure
children.text: The term “critical infrastructure” means, subject to regulations prescribed by the Committee, systems and assets, whether physical or virtual, so vital to the United States that the incapacity or destruction of such systems or assets would have a debilitating impact on national security.

children.label: i
children.text: Defense articles or defense services included on the United States Munitions List set forth in the International Traffic in Arms Regulations under subchapter M of chapter I of title 22, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.label: I
children.text: pursuant to multilateral regimes, including for reasons relating to national security, chemical and biological weapons proliferation, nuclear nonproliferation, or missile technology; or

children.label: II
children.text: for reasons relating to regional stability or surreptitious listening.

children.label: iii
children.text: Specially designed and prepared nuclear equipment, parts and components, materials, software, and technology covered by part 810 of title 10, Code of Federal Regulations (relating to assistance to foreign atomic energy activities).

children.label: iv
children.text: Nuclear facilities, equipment, and material covered by part 110 of title 10, Code of Federal Regulations (relating to export and import of nuclear equipment and material).

children.label: v
children.text: Select agents and toxins covered by part 331 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations, part 121 of title 9 of such Code, or part 73 of title 42 of such Code.

children.label: vi
children.text: Emerging and foundational technologies controlled pursuant to .section 4817 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The chairperson may recommend technologies for identification under the interagency process set forth in .section 4817(a) of this title

children.heading: Matters informing recommendations
children.label: ii Matters informing recommendations
children.text: Recommendations by the chairperson under clause (i) shall draw upon information arising from reviews and investigations conducted under subsection (b), notices submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i), declarations filed under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v), and non-notified and non-declared transactions identified under subsection (b)(1)(H).

children.heading: Foreign government-controlled transaction
children.label: 7 Foreign government-controlled transaction
children.text: The term “foreign government-controlled transaction” means any covered transaction that could result in the control of any United States business by a foreign government or an entity controlled by or acting on behalf of a foreign government.

children.heading: Intelligence community
children.label: 8 Intelligence community
children.text: The term “intelligence community” has the meaning given that term in .section 3003(4) of title 50

children.heading: Investment
children.label: 9 Investment
children.text: The term “investment” means the acquisition of equity interest, including contingent equity interest, as further defined in regulations prescribed by the Committee.

children.heading: Lead agency
children.label: 10 Lead agency
children.text: The term “lead agency” means the agency or agencies designated as the lead agency or agencies pursuant to subsection (k)(5).

children.heading: Party
children.label: 11 Party
children.text: The term “party” has the meaning given that term in regulations prescribed by the Committee.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 12 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means the several States, the District of Columbia, and any territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: United States business
children.label: 13 United States business
children.text: The term “United States business” means a person engaged in interstate commerce in the United States.

children.heading: National security reviews and investigations
children.label: b National security reviews and investigations
i
children.text: shall review the covered transaction to determine the effects of the transaction on the national security of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall consider the factors specified in subsection (f) for such purpose, as appropriate.

children.heading: Control by foreign government
children.label: B Control by foreign government
children.text: If the Committee determines that the covered transaction is a foreign government-controlled transaction, the Committee shall conduct an investigation of the transaction under paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Any party or parties to any covered transaction may initiate a review of the transaction under this paragraph by submitting a written notice of the transaction to the Chairperson of the Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: Subject to item (cc), the Committee shall provide comments on a draft or formal written notice or accept a formal written notice submitted under subclause (I) with respect to a covered transaction not later than the date that is 10 business days after the date of submission of the draft or formal written notice.

children.heading: Completeness
children.label: bb Completeness
children.text: If the Committee determines that a draft or formal written notice described in item (aa) is not complete, the Committee shall notify the party or parties to the transaction in writing that the notice is not complete and provide an explanation of all material respects in which the notice is incomplete.

children.heading: Stipulations required
children.label: cc Stipulations required
children.text: The timing requirement under item (aa) shall apply only in a case in which the parties stipulate under clause (vi) that the transaction is a covered transaction.

children.heading: Withdrawal of notice
children.label: ii Withdrawal of notice
children.text: No covered transaction for which a notice was submitted under clause (i) may be withdrawn from review, unless a written request for such withdrawal is submitted to the Committee by any party to the transaction and approved by the Committee.

children.heading: Continuing discussions
children.label: iii Continuing discussions
children.text: A request for withdrawal under clause (ii) shall not be construed to preclude any party to the covered transaction from continuing informal discussions with the Committee or any member thereof regarding possible resubmission for review pursuant to this paragraph.

children.heading: Inclusion of partnership and side agreements
children.label: iv Inclusion of partnership and side agreements
children.text: The Committee may require a written notice submitted under clause (i) to include a copy of any partnership agreements, integration agreements, or other side agreements relating to the transaction, as specified in regulations prescribed by the Committee.

children.heading: Declarations for certain covered transactions
children.label: v Declarations for certain covered transactions

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A party to any covered transaction may submit to the Committee a declaration with basic information regarding the transaction instead of a written notice under clause (i).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: II Regulations
children.text: The Committee shall prescribe regulations establishing requirements for declarations submitted under this clause. In prescribing such regulations, the Committee shall ensure that such declarations are submitted as abbreviated notifications that would not generally exceed 5 pages in length.

children.label: AA
children.text: request that the parties to the transaction file a written notice under clause (i);

children.label: BB
children.text: inform the parties to the transaction that the Committee is not able to complete action under this section with respect to the transaction on the basis of the declaration and that the parties may file a written notice under clause (i) to seek written notification from the Committee that the Committee has completed all action under this section with respect to the transaction;

children.label: CC
children.text: initiate a unilateral review of the transaction under subparagraph (D); or

children.label: DD
children.text: notify the parties in writing that the Committee has completed all action under this section with respect to the transaction.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: bb Timing
children.text: The Committee shall take action under item (aa) not later than 30 days after receiving a declaration under this clause.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: cc Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subclause (other than item (aa)(CC)) shall be construed to affect the authority of the President or the Committee to take any action authorized by this section with respect to a covered transaction.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: aa Regulations
children.text: The Committee shall prescribe regulations specifying the types of covered transactions for which the Committee requires a declaration under this subclause.

children.heading: Certain covered transactions with foreign government interests
children.label: bb Certain covered transactions with foreign government interests

children.heading: In general
children.label: AA In general
children.text: Except as provided in subitem (BB), the parties to a covered transaction shall submit a declaration described in subclause (I) with respect to the transaction if the transaction involves an investment that results in the acquisition, directly or indirectly, of a substantial interest in a United States business described in subsection (a)(4)(B)(iii) by a foreign person in which a foreign government has, directly or indirectly, a substantial interest.

children.heading: Substantial interest defined
children.label: BB Substantial interest defined
children.text: In this item, the term “substantial interest” has the meaning given that term in regulations which the Committee shall prescribe. In developing those regulations, the Committee shall consider the means by which a foreign government could influence the actions of a foreign person, including through board membership, ownership interest, or shareholder rights. An interest that is excluded under subparagraph (D) of subsection (a)(4) from the term “other investment” as used in subparagraph (B)(iii) of that subsection or that is less than a 10 percent voting interest shall not be considered a substantial interest.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: CC Waiver
children.text: The Committee may waive, with respect to a foreign person, the requirement under subitem (AA) for the submission of a declaration described in subclause (I) if the Committee determines that the foreign person demonstrates that the investments of the foreign person are not directed by a foreign government and the foreign person has a history of cooperation with the Committee.

children.heading: Other declarations required by Committee
children.label: cc Other declarations required by Committee
children.text: The Committee may require the submission of a declaration described in subclause (I) with respect to any covered transaction identified under regulations prescribed by the Committee for purposes of this item, at the discretion of the Committee, that involves a United States business described in subsection (a)(4)(B)(iii)(II).

children.label: AA
children.text: the fund is managed exclusively by a general partner, a managing member, or an equivalent;

children.label: BB
children.text: the general partner, managing member, or equivalent is not a foreign person; and

children.label: CC
children.text: the investment fund satisfies, with respect to any foreign person with membership as a limited partner on an advisory board or a committee of the fund, the criteria specified in items (cc) and (dd) of subsection (a)(4)(D)(iv).

children.heading: Submission of written notice as an alternative
children.label: ee Submission of written notice as an alternative
children.text: Parties to a covered transaction for which a declaration is required under this subclause may instead elect to submit a written notice under clause (i).

children.heading: Timing and refiling of submission
children.label: ff Timing and refiling of submission

children.heading: In general
children.label: AA In general
children.text: In the regulations prescribed under item (aa), the Committee may not require a declaration to be submitted under this subclause with respect to a covered transaction more than 45 days before the completion of the transaction.

children.heading: Refiling of declaration
children.label: BB Refiling of declaration
children.text: The Committee may not request or recommend that a declaration submitted under this subclause be withdrawn and refiled, except to permit parties to a covered transaction to correct material errors or omissions in the declaration submitted with respect to that transaction.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: gg Penalties
children.text: The Committee may impose a penalty pursuant to subsection (h)(3) with respect to a party that fails to comply with this subclause.

children.label: aa
children.text: stipulate that the transaction is a covered transaction; and

children.label: bb
children.text: if the party stipulates that the transaction is a covered transaction under item (aa), stipulate that the transaction is a foreign government-controlled transaction.

children.heading: Basis for stipulation
children.label: II Basis for stipulation
children.text: A written notice submitted under clause (i) or a declaration submitted under clause (v) that includes a stipulation under subclause (I) shall include a description of the basis for the stipulation.

children.label: i
children.text: any covered transaction (other than a covered transaction described in subparagraph (E));

children.label: ii

children.label: I
children.text: any party to the transaction or the entity resulting from consummation of the transaction materially breaches a mitigation agreement or condition described in subsection ()(3)(A);l

children.label: II
children.text: such breach is certified to the Committee by the lead department or agency monitoring and enforcing such agreement or condition as a material breach; and

children.label: III
children.text: the Committee determines that there are no other adequate and appropriate remedies or enforcement tools available to address such breach.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee has informed the parties to the transaction in writing that the Committee has completed all action under this section with respect to the transaction; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the President has announced a decision not to exercise the President’s authority under subsection (d) with respect to the transaction.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: F Timing
children.text: Any review under this paragraph shall be completed before the end of the 45-day period beginning on the date of the acceptance of written notice under subparagraph (C) by the chairperson, or beginning on the date of the initiation of the review in accordance with subparagraph (D), as applicable.

children.heading: Limit on delegation of certain authority
children.label: G Limit on delegation of certain authority
children.text: The authority of the Committee to initiate a review under subparagraph (D) may not be delegated to any person, other than the Deputy Secretary or an appropriate Under Secretary of the department or agency represented on the Committee.

children.heading: Identification of non-notified and non-declared transactions
children.label: H Identification of non-notified and non-declared transactions
i
children.text: a notice under clause (i) of subparagraph (C) or a declaration under clause (v) of that subparagraph is not submitted to the Committee; and

children.label: ii
children.text: information is reasonably available.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In each case described in subparagraph (B), the Committee shall immediately conduct an investigation of the effects of a covered transaction on the national security of the United States, and take any necessary actions in connection with the transaction to protect the national security of the United States.

children.label: I
children.text: the transaction threatens to impair the national security of the United States and the risk has not been mitigated during or prior to the review of a covered transaction under paragraph (1);

children.label: II
children.text: the transaction is a foreign government-controlled transaction; or

children.label: III
children.text: the transaction would result in control of any critical infrastructure of or within the United States by or on behalf of any foreign person, if the Committee determines that the transaction could impair national security, and that such impairment to national security has not been mitigated by assurances provided or renewed with the approval of the Committee, as described in subsection (), during the review period under paragraph (1); orl

children.label: ii
children.text: the lead agency recommends, and the Committee concurs, that an investigation be undertaken.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), any investigation under subparagraph (A) shall be completed before the end of the 45-day period beginning on the date on which the investigation commenced.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In extraordinary circumstances (as defined by the Committee in regulations), the chairperson may, at the request of the head of the lead agency, extend an investigation under subparagraph (A) for one 15-day period.

children.heading: Nondelegation
children.label: II Nondelegation
children.text: The authority of the chairperson and the head of the lead agency referred to in subclause (I) may not be delegated to any person other than the Deputy Secretary of the Treasury or the deputy head (or equivalent thereof) of the lead agency, as the case may be.

children.heading: Notification to parties
children.label: III Notification to parties
children.text: If the Committee extends the deadline under subclause (I) with respect to a covered transaction, the Committee shall notify the parties to the transaction of the extension.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (B)(i), an investigation of a foreign government-controlled transaction described in subclause (II) of subparagraph (B)(i) or a transaction involving critical infrastructure described in subclause (III) of subparagraph (B)(i) shall not be required under this paragraph, if the Secretary of the Treasury and the head of the lead agency jointly determine, on the basis of the review of the transaction under paragraph (1), that the transaction will not impair the national security of the United States.

children.heading: Nondelegation
children.label: ii Nondelegation
children.text: The authority of the Secretary or the head of an agency referred to in clause (i) may not be delegated to any person, other than the Deputy Secretary of the Treasury or the deputy head (or the equivalent thereof) of the lead agency, respectively.

children.heading: Guidance on certain transactions with national security implications
children.label: E Guidance on certain transactions with national security implications
children.text: The Chairperson shall, not later than 180 days after the effective date of the Foreign Investment and National Security Act of 2007, publish in the Federal Register guidance on the types of transactions that the Committee has reviewed and that have presented national security considerations, including transactions that may constitute covered transactions that would result in control of critical infrastructure relating to United States national security by a foreign government or an entity controlled by or acting on behalf of a foreign government.

children.heading: Certified notice at completion of review or assessment
children.label: A Certified notice at completion of review or assessment
children.text: Upon completion of a review under this subsection that concludes action under this section, or upon the Committee making a notification under paragraph (1)(C)(v)(III)(aa)(DD), the chairperson and the head of the lead agency shall transmit a certified notice to the members of Congress specified in subparagraph (C)(iii).

children.heading: Certified report at completion of investigation
children.label: B Certified report at completion of investigation
children.text: As soon as is practicable after completion of an investigation under subsection (b) that concludes action under this section, the chairperson and the head of the lead agency shall transmit to the members of Congress specified in subparagraph (C)(iii) a certified written report (consistent with the requirements of subsection (c)) on the results of the investigation, unless the matter under investigation has been sent to the President for decision.

children.label: I
children.text: a description of the actions taken by the Committee with respect to the transaction;

children.label: II
children.text: a certification that all relevant national security factors have received full consideration; and

children.label: III
children.text: whether the transaction is described under clause (i), (ii), (iii), (iv), or (v) of subsection (a)(4)(B).

children.heading: Content of certification
children.label: ii Content of certification
children.text: Each certified notice and report required under subparagraphs (A) and (B), respectively, shall be signed by the chairperson and the head of the lead agency, and shall state that, in the determination of the Committee, there are no unresolved national security concerns with the transaction that is the subject of the notice or report.

children.label: I
children.text: to the Majority Leader and the Minority Leader of the Senate;

children.label: II
children.text: to the chair and ranking member of the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and of any committee of the Senate having oversight over the lead agency;

children.label: III

children.label: IV
children.text: to the chair and ranking member of the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives and of any committee of the House of Representatives having oversight over the lead agency; and

children.label: V
children.text: with respect to covered transactions involving critical infrastructure, to the members of the Senate from the State in which the principal place of business of the acquired United States person is located, and the member from the Congressional District in which such principal place of business is located.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Each certified notice and report required under subparagraphs (A) and (B), respectively, shall be signed by the chairperson and the head of the lead agency, which signature requirement may only be delegated in accordance with subclause (II).

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: Subject to item (bb), the chairperson, in consultation with the Committee, may determine the level of official to whom the signature requirement under subclause (I) for the chairperson and the head of the lead agency may be delegated. The level of official to whom the signature requirement may be delegated may differ based on any factor relating to a transaction that the chairperson, in consultation with the Committee, deems appropriate, including the type or value of the transaction.

children.heading: Limitation on delegation with respect to certain transactions
children.label: bb Limitation on delegation with respect to certain transactions
children.text: The signature requirement under subclause (I) may be delegated not below the level of the Assistant Secretary of the Treasury or an equivalent official of the lead agency.

children.heading: Authority to consolidate documents
children.label: v Authority to consolidate documents
children.text: Instead of transmitting a separate certified notice or certified report under subparagraph (A) or (B) with respect to each covered transaction, the Committee may, on a monthly basis, transmit such notices and reports in a consolidated document to the Members of Congress specified in clause (iii).

children.heading: Analysis by Director of National Intelligence
children.label: 4 Analysis by Director of National Intelligence

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Director of National Intelligence shall expeditiously carry out a thorough analysis of any threat to the national security of the United States posed by any covered transaction, which shall include the identification of any recognized gaps in the collection of intelligence relevant to the analysis.

children.heading: Views of intelligence community
children.label: ii Views of intelligence community
children.text: The Director shall seek and incorporate into the analysis required by clause (i) the views of all affected or appropriate agencies of the intelligence community with respect to the transaction.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: iii Updates
children.text: At the request of the lead agency, the Director shall update the analysis conducted under clause (i) with respect to a covered transaction with respect to which an agreement was entered into under subsection ()(3)(A).l

children.heading: Independence and objectivity
children.label: iv Independence and objectivity
children.text: The Committee shall ensure that its processes under this section preserve the ability of the Director to conduct analysis under clause (i) that is independent, objective, and consistent with all applicable directives, policies, and analytic tradecraft standards of the intelligence community.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence may provide the Committee with basic information regarding any threat to the national security of the United States posed by a covered transaction described in clause (ii) instead of conducting the analysis required by subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: the transaction is described in subsection (a)(4)(B)(ii);

children.label: II
children.text: the Director of National Intelligence has completed an analysis pursuant to subparagraph (A) involving each foreign person that is a party to the transaction during the 12 months preceding the review or investigation of the transaction under this section; or

children.label: III
children.text: the transaction otherwise meets criteria agreed upon by the Committee and the Director for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: C Timing
children.text: The analysis required under subparagraph (A) shall be provided by the Director of National Intelligence to the Committee not later than 30 days after the date on which notice of the transaction is accepted by the Committee under paragraph (1)(C), but such analysis may be supplemented or amended, as the Director considers necessary or appropriate, or upon a request for additional information by the Committee. The Director may begin the analysis at any time prior to acceptance of the notice, in accordance with otherwise applicable law.

children.heading: Interaction with intelligence community
children.label: D Interaction with intelligence community
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence shall ensure that the intelligence community remains engaged in the collection, analysis, and dissemination to the Committee of any additional relevant information that may become available during the course of any investigation conducted under subsection (b) with respect to a transaction.

children.heading: Independent role of Director
children.label: E Independent role of Director
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence shall be a nonvoting, ex officio member of the Committee, and shall be provided with all notices received by the Committee under paragraph (1)(C) regarding covered transactions, but shall serve no policy role on the Committee, other than to provide analysis under subparagraphs (A) and (C) in connection with a covered transaction.

children.heading: Assessment of operational impact
children.label: F Assessment of operational impact
children.text: The Director may provide to the Committee an assessment, separate from the analyses under subparagraphs (A) and (B), of any operational impact of a covered transaction on the intelligence community and a description of any actions that have been or will be taken to mitigate any such impact.

children.heading: Submission to Congress
children.label: G Submission to Congress
children.text: The Committee shall submit the analysis required by subparagraph (A) with respect to a covered transaction to the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives upon the conclusion of action under this section (other than compliance plans under subsection ()(6)) with respect to the transaction.l

children.heading: Submission of additional information
children.label: 5 Submission of additional information
children.text: No provision of this subsection shall be construed as prohibiting any party to a covered transaction from submitting additional information concerning the transaction, including any proposed restructuring of the transaction or any modifications to any agreements in connection with the transaction, while any review or investigation of the transaction is ongoing.

children.heading: Notice of results to parties
children.label: 6 Notice of results to parties
children.text: The Committee shall notify the parties to a covered transaction of the results of a review or investigation under this section, promptly upon completion of all action under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: submitting any notice of a covered transaction to the Committee;

children.label: B
children.text: submitting a request to withdraw a covered transaction from review;

children.label: C
children.text: resubmitting a notice of a covered transaction that was previously withdrawn from review; and

children.label: D
children.text: providing notice of the results of a review or investigation to the parties to the covered transaction, upon completion of all action under this section.

children.heading: Tolling of deadlines during lapse in appropriations
children.label: 8 Tolling of deadlines during lapse in appropriations
children.text: Any deadline or time limitation under this subsection shall be tolled during a lapse in appropriations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), any information or documentary material filed with the President or the President’s designee pursuant to this section shall be exempt from disclosure under , and no such information or documentary material may be made public.section 552 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: Information relevant to any administrative or judicial action or proceeding.

children.label: B
children.text: Information to Congress or any duly authorized committee or subcommittee of Congress.

children.label: C
children.text: Information important to the national security analysis or actions of the Committee to any domestic governmental entity, or to any foreign governmental entity of a United States ally or partner, under the exclusive direction and authorization of the chairperson, only to the extent necessary for national security purposes, and subject to appropriate confidentiality and classification requirements.

children.label: D
children.text: Information that the parties have consented to be disclosed to third parties.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The chairperson, in consultation with other members of the Committee, should establish a formal process for the exchange of information under paragraph (2)(C) with governments of countries that are allies or partners of the United States, in the discretion of the chairperson, to protect the national security of the United States and those countries.

children.label: i
children.text: be designed to facilitate the harmonization of action with respect to trends in investment and technology that could pose risks to the national security of the United States and countries that are allies or partners of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: provide for the sharing of information with respect to specific technologies and entities acquiring such technologies as appropriate to ensure national security; and

children.label: iii
children.text: include consultations and meetings with representatives of the governments of such countries on a recurring basis.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), the President may take such action for such time as the President considers appropriate to suspend or prohibit any covered transaction that threatens to impair the national security of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which the investigation of the transaction under subsection (b) is completed; or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the Committee otherwise refers the transaction to the President under subsection ()(2).l

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 3 Enforcement
children.text: The President may direct the Attorney General of the United States to seek appropriate relief, including divestment relief, in the district courts of the United States, in order to implement and enforce this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: there is credible evidence that leads the President to believe that a foreign person that would acquire an interest in a United States business or its assets as a result of the covered transaction might take action that threatens to impair the national security; and

children.label: B
children.text: provisions of law, other than this section and the International Emergency Economic Powers Act [ et seq.], do not, in the judgment of the President, provide adequate and appropriate authority for the President to protect the national security in the matter before the President.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Factors to be considered
children.label: 5 Factors to be considered
children.text: For purposes of determining whether to take action under paragraph (1), the President shall consider, among other factors each of the factors described in subsection (f), as appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The actions of the President under paragraph (1) of subsection (d) and the findings of the President under paragraph (4) of subsection (d) shall not be subject to judicial review.

children.heading: Civil actions
children.label: 2 Civil actions
children.text: A civil action challenging an action or finding under this section may be brought only in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.

children.heading: Procedures for review of privileged information
children.label: 3 Procedures for review of privileged information
children.text: If a civil action challenging an action or finding under this section is brought, and the court determines that protected information in the administrative record, including classified or other information subject to privilege or protections under any provision of law, is necessary to resolve the challenge, that information shall be submitted ex parte and in camera to the court and the court shall maintain that information under seal.

children.heading: Applicability of use of information provisions
children.label: 4 Applicability of use of information provisions
children.text: The use of information provisions of sections 1806, 1825, 1845, and 1881e of this title shall not apply in a civil action brought under this subsection.
domestic production needed for projected national defense requirements,

the capability and capacity of domestic industries to meet national defense requirements, including the availability of human resources, products, technology, materials, and other supplies and services,
the control of domestic industries and commercial activity by foreign citizens as it affects the capability and capacity of the United States to meet the requirements of national security,

children.label: C

the potential effects of the proposed or pending transaction on United States international technological leadership in areas affecting United States national security;
the potential national security-related effects on United States critical infrastructure, including major energy assets;
the potential national security-related effects on United States critical technologies;
whether the covered transaction is a foreign government-controlled transaction, as determined under subsection (b)(1)(B);

children.label: A
children.text: the adherence of the subject country to nonproliferation control regimes, including treaties and multilateral supply guidelines, which shall draw on, but not be limited to, the annual report on “Adherence to and Compliance with Arms Control, Nonproliferation and Disarmament Agreements and Commitments” required by ;section 2593a of title 22

children.label: B
children.text: the relationship of such country with the United States, specifically on its record on cooperating in counter-terrorism efforts, which shall draw on, but not be limited to, the report of the President to Congress under section 7120 of the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004; and

children.label: C
children.text: the potential for transshipment or diversion of technologies with military applications, including an analysis of national export control laws and regulations;
the long-term projection of United States requirements for sources of energy and other critical resources and material; and
such other factors as the President or the Committee may determine to be appropriate, generally or in connection with a specific review or investigation.

children.heading: Additional information to Congress; confidentiality
children.label: g Additional information to Congress; confidentiality

children.heading: Briefing requirement on request
children.label: 1 Briefing requirement on request
children.text: The Committee shall, upon request from any Member of Congress specified in subsection (b)(3)(C)(iii), promptly provide briefings on a covered transaction for which all action has concluded under this section, or on compliance with a mitigation agreement or condition imposed with respect to such transaction, on a classified basis, if deemed necessary by the sensitivity of the information. Briefings under this paragraph may be provided to the congressional staff of such a Member of Congress having appropriate security clearance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The disclosure of information under this subsection shall be consistent with the requirements of subsection (c). Members of Congress and staff of either House of Congress or any committee of Congress, shall be subject to the same limitations on disclosure of information as are applicable under subsection (c).

children.heading: Proprietary information
children.label: B Proprietary information
children.text: Proprietary information which can be associated with a particular party to a covered transaction shall be furnished in accordance with subparagraph (A) only to a committee of Congress, and only when the committee provides assurances of confidentiality, unless such party otherwise consents in writing to such disclosure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall direct, subject to notice and comment, the issuance of regulations to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: provide for the imposition of civil penalties for any violation of this section, including any mitigation agreement entered into, conditions imposed, or order issued pursuant to this section;

children.label: i
children.text: minimize paperwork burdens; and

children.label: ii
children.text: coordinate reporting requirements under this section with reporting requirements under any other provision of Federal law;

children.label: C
children.text: provide for an appropriate role for the Secretary of Labor with respect to mitigation agreements; and

children.label: i
children.text: consider the factors specified in subsection (f); and

children.label: ii
children.text: as appropriate, require parties to provide to the Committee the information necessary to consider such factors.

children.heading: Effect on other law
children.label: i Effect on other law
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed as altering or affecting any other authority, process, regulation, investigation, enforcement measure, or review provided by or established under any other provision of Federal law, including the International Emergency Economic Powers Act [ et seq.], or any other authority of the President or the Congress under the Constitution of the United States.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Technology risk assessments
children.label: j Technology risk assessments
children.text: In any case in which an assessment of the risk of diversion of defense critical technology is performed by a designee of the President, a copy of such assessment shall be provided to any other designee of the President responsible for reviewing or investigating a transaction under this section.

children.heading: Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States
children.label: k Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States, established pursuant to Executive Order No. 11858, shall be a multi agency committee to carry out this section and such other assignments as the President may designate.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.label: C
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce.

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary of Defense.

children.label: E
children.text: The Secretary of State.

children.label: F
children.text: The Attorney General of the United States.

children.label: G
children.text: The Secretary of Energy.

children.label: H
children.text: The Secretary of Labor (nonvoting, ex officio).

children.label: I
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence (nonvoting, ex officio).

children.label: J
children.text: The heads of any other executive department, agency, or office, as the President determines appropriate, generally or on a case-by-case basis.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 3 Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall serve as the chairperson of the Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each member of the Committee shall designate an Assistant Secretary, or an equivalent official, who is appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, to carry out such duties related to the Committee as the member of the Committee may delegate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: There shall be established in the Office of International Affairs at the Department of the Treasury 2 additional positions of Assistant Secretary of the Treasury, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, to carry out such duties related to the Committee as the Secretary of the Treasury may delegate, consistent with this section.

children.heading: Assistant Secretary for Investment Security
children.label: II Assistant Secretary for Investment Security
children.text: One of the positions of Assistant Secretary of the Treasury authorized under subclause (I) shall be the Assistant Secretary for Investment Security, whose duties shall be principally related to the Committee, as delegated by the Secretary of the Treasury under this section.

children.heading: Special hiring authority
children.label: B Special hiring authority
children.text: The heads of the departments and agencies represented on the Committee may appoint, without regard to the provisions of sections 3309 through 3318 of title 5, candidates directly to positions in the competitive service (as defined in section 2102 of that title) in their respective departments and agencies. The primary responsibility of positions authorized under the preceding sentence shall be to administer this section.

children.label: A
children.text: for each covered transaction, and for negotiating any mitigation agreements or other conditions necessary to protect national security; and

children.label: B
children.text: for all matters related to the monitoring of the completed transaction, to ensure compliance with such agreements or conditions and with this section.

children.heading: Other members
children.label: 6 Other members
children.text: The chairperson shall consult with the heads of such other Federal departments, agencies, and independent establishments in any review or investigation under subsection (a), as the chairperson determines to be appropriate, on the basis of the facts and circumstances of the covered transaction under review or investigation (or the designee of any such department or agency head).

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 7 Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall meet upon the direction of the President or upon the call of the chairperson, without regard to (if otherwise applicable).section 552b of title 5

children.heading: Actions by the Committee to address national security risks
children.label: l Actions by the Committee to address national security risks

children.heading: Suspension of transactions
children.label: 1 Suspension of transactions
children.text: The Committee, acting through the chairperson, may suspend a proposed or pending covered transaction that may pose a risk to the national security of the United States for such time as the covered transaction is under review or investigation under subsection (b).

children.heading: Referral to President
children.label: 2 Referral to President
children.text: The Committee may, at any time during the review or investigation of a covered transaction under subsection (b), complete the action of the Committee with respect to the transaction and refer the transaction to the President for action pursuant to subsection (d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Committee or a lead agency may, on behalf of the Committee, negotiate, enter into or impose, and enforce any agreement or condition with any party to the covered transaction in order to mitigate any risk to the national security of the United States that arises as a result of the covered transaction.

children.heading: Abandonment of transactions
children.label: ii Abandonment of transactions
children.text: If a party to a covered transaction has voluntarily chosen to abandon the transaction, the Committee or lead agency, as the case may be, may negotiate, enter into or impose, and enforce any agreement or condition with any party to the covered transaction for purposes of effectuating such abandonment and mitigating any risk to the national security of the United States that arises as a result of the covered transaction.

children.heading: Agreements and conditions relating to completed transactions
children.label: iii Agreements and conditions relating to completed transactions
children.text: The Committee or lead agency, as the case may be, may negotiate, enter into or impose, and enforce any agreement or condition with any party to a completed covered transaction in order to mitigate any interim risk to the national security of the United States that may arise as a result of the covered transaction until such time that the Committee has completed action pursuant to subsection (b) or the President has taken action pursuant to subsection (d) with respect to the transaction.

children.heading: Treatment of outdated agreements or conditions
children.label: B Treatment of outdated agreements or conditions
children.text: The chairperson and the head of the lead agency shall periodically review the appropriateness of an agreement or condition imposed under subparagraph (A) and terminate, phase out, or otherwise amend the agreement or condition if a threat no longer requires mitigation through the agreement or condition.

children.label: i
children.text: be effective;

children.label: ii
children.text: allow for compliance with the terms of the agreement or condition in an appropriately verifiable way; and

children.label: iii
children.text: enable effective monitoring of compliance with and enforcement of the terms of the agreement or condition.

children.heading: Jurisdiction
children.label: D Jurisdiction
children.text: The provisions of shall apply to any mitigation agreement entered into or condition imposed under subparagraph (A).section 4556(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any determination of the Committee to suspend a covered transaction under paragraph (1), to refer a covered transaction to the President under paragraph (2), or to negotiate, enter into or impose, or enforce any agreement or condition under paragraph (3)(A) with respect to a covered transaction, shall be based on a risk-based analysis, conducted by the Committee, of the effects on the national security of the United States of the covered transaction, which shall include an assessment of the threat, vulnerabilities, and consequences to national security related to the transaction.

children.heading: Actions of members of the Committee
children.label: B Actions of members of the Committee

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any member of the Committee who concludes that a covered transaction poses an unresolved national security concern shall recommend to the Committee that the Committee suspend the transaction under paragraph (1), refer the transaction to the President under paragraph (2), or negotiate, enter into or impose, or enforce any agreement or condition under paragraph (3)(A) with respect to the transaction. In making that recommendation, the member shall propose or contribute to the risk-based analysis required by subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: a written statement justifying the alternative recommendation; and

children.label: II
children.text: as appropriate, a risk-based analysis that supports the alternative recommendation.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: C Definitions
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the terms “threat”, “vulnerabilities”, and “consequences to national security” shall have the meanings given those terms by the Committee by regulation.

children.label: i
children.text: interim protections to address specific concerns with such transaction that have been raised in connection with any such review or investigation pending any resubmission of any written notice under this section with respect to such transaction and further action by the President under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: specific time frames for resubmitting any such written notice; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a process for tracking any actions that may be taken by any party to the transaction, in connection with the transaction, before the notice referred to in clause (ii) is resubmitted.

children.heading: Designation of agency
children.label: B Designation of agency
children.text: The lead agency, other than any entity of the intelligence community, shall, on behalf of the Committee, ensure that the requirements of subparagraph (A) with respect to any covered transaction that is subject to such subparagraph are met.

children.heading: Designation of lead agency
children.label: A Designation of lead agency
children.text: The lead agency shall negotiate, modify, monitor, and enforce, on behalf of the Committee, any agreement entered into or condition imposed under paragraph (3) with respect to a covered transaction, based on the expertise with and knowledge of the issues related to such transaction on the part of the designated department or agency. The lead agency may, at its discretion, seek and receive the assistance of other departments or agencies in carrying out the purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: provide periodic reports to the Committee on any material modification to any such agreement or condition imposed with respect to the transaction; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that any material modification to any such agreement or condition is reported to the Director of National Intelligence, the Attorney General of the United States, and any other Federal department or agency that may have a material interest in such modification.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a covered transaction with respect to which an agreement is entered into under paragraph (3)(A), the Committee or lead agency, as the case may be, shall formulate, adhere to, and keep updated a plan for monitoring compliance with the agreement.

children.label: I
children.text: which member of the Committee will have primary responsibility for monitoring compliance with the agreement;

children.label: II
children.text: how compliance with the agreement will be monitored;

children.label: III
children.text: how frequently compliance reviews will be conducted;

children.label: IV
children.text: whether an independent entity will be utilized under subparagraph (E) to conduct compliance reviews; and

children.label: V
children.text: what actions will be taken if the parties fail to cooperate regarding monitoring compliance with the agreement.

children.heading: Effect of lack of compliance
children.label: D Effect of lack of compliance
i
children.text: negotiate a plan of action for the party or parties to remediate the lack of compliance, with failure to abide by the plan or otherwise remediate the lack of compliance serving as the basis for the Committee to find a material breach of the agreement or condition;

children.label: ii
children.text: require that the party or parties submit a written notice under clause (i) of subsection (b)(1)(C) or a declaration under clause (v) of that subsection with respect to a covered transaction initiated after the date of the determination of noncompliance and before the date that is 5 years after the date of the determination to the Committee to initiate a review of the transaction under subsection (b); or

children.label: iii
children.text: seek injunctive relief.

children.heading: Use of independent entities to monitor compliance
children.label: E Use of independent entities to monitor compliance
children.text: If the parties to an agreement entered into under paragraph (3)(A) enter into a contract with an independent entity from outside the United States Government for the purpose of monitoring compliance with the agreement, the Committee shall take such action as is necessary to prevent a conflict of interest from arising by ensuring that the independent entity owes no fiduciary duty to the parties.

children.heading: Successors and assigns
children.label: F Successors and assigns
children.text: Any agreement or condition entered into or imposed under paragraph (3)(A) shall be considered binding on all successors and assigns unless and until the agreement or condition terminates on its own terms or is otherwise terminated by the Committee in its sole discretion.

children.heading: Additional compliance measures
children.label: G Additional compliance measures
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (A) through (F), the Committee shall develop and agree upon methods for evaluating compliance with any agreement entered into or condition imposed with respect to a covered transaction that will allow the Committee to adequately ensure compliance without unnecessarily diverting Committee resources from assessing any new covered transaction for which a written notice under clause (i) of subsection (b)(1)(C) or declaration under clause (v) of that subsection has been filed, and if necessary, reaching a mitigation agreement with or imposing a condition on a party to such covered transaction or any covered transaction for which a review has been reopened for any reason.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The chairperson shall transmit a report to the chairman and ranking member of the committee of jurisdiction in the Senate and the House of Representatives, before July 31 of each year on all of the reviews and investigations of covered transactions completed under subsection (b) during the 12-month period covered by the report.

children.heading: Contents of report relating to covered transactions
children.label: 2 Contents of report relating to covered transactions
i
children.text: a description of the outcome of each review or investigation, including whether an agreement was entered into or condition was imposed under subsection ()(3)(A) with respect to the transaction being reviewed or investigated, and whether the President took any action under this section with respect to that transaction;l

children.label: ii
children.text: basic information on each party to each such transaction;

children.label: iii
children.text: the nature of the business activities or products of the United States business with which the transaction was entered into or intended to be entered into; and

children.label: iv
children.text: information about any withdrawal from the process.

children.label: B
children.text: Specific, cumulative, and, as appropriate, trend information on the numbers of filings, investigations, withdrawals, and decisions or actions by the President under this section.

children.label: C
children.text: Cumulative and, as appropriate, trend information on the business sectors involved in the filings which have been made, and the countries from which the investments have originated.

children.label: D
children.text: Information on whether companies that withdrew notices to the Committee in accordance with subsection (b)(1)(C)(ii) have later refiled such notices, or, alternatively, abandoned the transaction.

children.label: E
children.text: The types of security arrangements and conditions the Committee has used to mitigate national security concerns about a transaction, including a discussion of the methods that the Committee and any lead agency are using to determine compliance with such arrangements or conditions.

children.label: F
children.text: A detailed discussion of all perceived adverse effects of covered transactions on the national security or critical infrastructure of the United States that the Committee will take into account in its deliberations during the period before delivery of the next report, to the extent possible.

children.label: G
children.text: Statistics on compliance plans conducted and actions taken by the Committee under subsection ()(6), including subparagraph (D) of that subsection, during that period, a general assessment of the compliance of parties with agreements entered into and conditions imposed under subsection ()(3)(A) that are in effect during that period, including a description of any actions taken by the Committee to impose penalties or initiate a unilateral review pursuant to subsection (b)(1)(D)(iii), and any recommendations for improving the enforcement of such agreements and conditions.ll

children.label: H
children.text: Cumulative and, as appropriate, trend information on the number of declarations filed under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v), the actions taken by the Committee in response to those declarations, the business sectors involved in those declarations, and the countries involved in those declarations.

children.label: i
children.text: the methods used by the Committee to identify non-notified and non-declared transactions under subsection (b)(1)(H);

children.label: ii
children.text: potential methods to improve such identification and the resources required to do so; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of transactions identified through the process established under that subsection during the reporting period and the number of such transactions flagged for further review.

children.label: J
children.text: A summary of the hiring practices and policies of the Committee pursuant to subsection (k)(4).

children.label: K
children.text: A list of the waivers granted by the Committee under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v)(IV)(bb)(CC).

children.label: L
children.text: Information on whether the most recent list of sites identified under subsection (a)(4)(C)(iii) reflects consideration of any recommended updates and revisions submitted under subclause (II) of that subsection. Upon request from the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives or the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate, the Committee shall provide to that committee a classified briefing regarding that list.

children.heading: Contents of report relating to critical technologies
children.label: 3 Contents of report relating to critical technologies
A
children.text: an evaluation of whether there is credible evidence of a coordinated strategy by 1 or more countries or companies to acquire United States companies involved in research, development, or production of critical technologies for which the United States is a leading producer;

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of whether there are industrial espionage activities directed or directly assisted by foreign governments against private United States companies aimed at obtaining commercial secrets related to critical technologies; and

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the technologies recommended by the chairperson under subsection (a)(6)(B) for identification under the interagency process set forth in .section 4817(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: All appropriate portions of the annual report under paragraph (1) may be classified. An unclassified version of the report, as appropriate, consistent with safeguarding national security and privacy, shall be made available to the public.

children.heading: Inclusion in classified version
children.label: B Inclusion in classified version
children.text: If the Committee recommends that the President suspend or prohibit a covered transaction because the transaction threatens to impair the national security of the United States, the Committee shall, in the classified version of the report required under paragraph (1), notify Congress of the recommendation and, upon request, provide a classified briefing on the recommendation.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of notices submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i);

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of declarations submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v) and the number of such declarations that were required under subclause (IV) of that subsection;

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of declarations submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v) for which the Committee required resubmission as notices under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i);

children.label: iv
children.text: the average number of days that elapsed between submission of a declaration under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v) and the acceptance of the declaration by the Committee;

children.label: v
children.text: the median and average number of days that elapsed between acceptance of a declaration by the Committee and a response described in subsection (b)(1)(C)(v)(III);

children.label: I
children.text: the average number of business days that elapsed between the date of submission of a draft notice and the date on which the Committee provided written comments on the draft notice;

children.label: II
children.text: the average number of business days that elapsed between the date of submission of a formal written notice and the date on which the Committee accepted or provided written comments on the formal written notice; and

children.label: aa
children.text: an explanation of the causes of such delays, including whether such delays are caused by resource shortages, unusual fluctuations in the volume of notices, transaction characteristics, or other factors; and

children.label: bb
children.text: an explanation of the steps that the Committee anticipates taking to mitigate the causes of such delays and otherwise to improve the ability of the Committee to provide comments on, or to accept, notices within 10 business days;

children.label: vii
children.text: the number of reviews or investigations conducted under subsection (b);

children.label: viii
children.text: the number of investigations that were subject to an extension under subsection (b)(2)(C)(ii);

children.label: ix
children.text: information on the duration of those reviews and investigations, including the median and average number of days required to complete those reviews and investigations;

children.label: x
children.text: the number of notices submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i) and declarations submitted under subsection (b)(1)(C)(v) that were rejected by the Committee;

children.label: xi
children.text: the number of such notices and declarations that were withdrawn by a party to the covered transaction;

children.label: xii
children.text: the number of such withdrawals that were followed by the submission of a subsequent such notice or declaration relating to a substantially similar covered transaction; and

children.label: xiii
children.text: such other specific, cumulative, or trend information that the Committee determines is advisable to provide for an assessment of the time required for reviews and investigations of covered transactions under this section.

children.heading: Certification of notices and assurances
children.label: n Certification of notices and assurances
A
children.text: the notice or information submitted fully complies with the requirements of this section or such regulation, agreement, or condition; and

children.label: B
children.text: the notice or information is accurate and complete in all material respects.

children.label: A
children.text: failed to submit a statement required by paragraph (1); or

children.label: B
children.text: included false or misleading information in a notice or information described in paragraph (1) or omitted material information from such notice or information.

children.heading: Applicability of law on fraud and false statements
children.label: 3 Applicability of law on fraud and false statements
children.text: The Committee shall prescribe regulations expressly providing for the application of , United States Code, to all information provided to the Committee under this section by any party to a covered transaction.section 1001 of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: anticipated resources necessary for operations of the Committee in the following fiscal year at each of the departments or agencies represented on the Committee;

children.label: i
children.text: ensure that thorough reviews and investigations are completed as expeditiously as possible;

children.label: ii
children.text: monitor and enforce mitigation agreements; and

children.label: iii
children.text: identify covered transactions for which a notice under clause (i) of subsection (b)(1)(C) or a declaration under clause (v) of that subsection was not submitted to the Committee;

children.label: C
children.text: management efforts to strengthen the ability of the Committee to meet the requirements of this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: educate the business community, with a particular focus on the technology sector and other sectors of importance to national security, on the goals and operations of the Committee;

children.label: I
children.text: strengthen national security reviews of relevant investment transactions; and

children.label: II
children.text: expedite such reviews when appropriate; and

children.label: iii
children.text: promote openness to foreign investment, consistent with national security considerations.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 2 Sunset
children.text: This subsection shall have no force or effect on or after the date that is 7 years after .August 13, 2018

children.heading: Establishment of Fund
children.label: 1 Establishment of Fund
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund, to be known as the “Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States Fund” (in this subsection referred to as the “Fund”), to be administered by the chairperson.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations for the Committee
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations for the Committee
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Fund for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023 $20,000,000 to perform the functions of the Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Committee may assess and collect a fee in an amount determined by the Committee in regulations, to the extent provided in advance in appropriations Acts, without regard to , and subject to subparagraph (B), with respect to each covered transaction for which a written notice is submitted to the Committee under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i). The total amount of fees collected under this paragraph may not exceed the costs of administering this section.section 9701 of title 31

children.heading: Determination of amount of fee
children.label: B Determination of amount of fee
aa
children.text: 1 percent of the value of the transaction; or

children.label: bb
children.text: $300,000, adjusted annually for inflation pursuant to regulations prescribed by the Committee; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the effect of the fee on small business concerns (as defined in );section 632 of title 15

children.label: bb
children.text: the expenses of the Committee associated with conducting activities under this section;

children.label: cc
children.text: the effect of the fee on foreign investment; and

children.label: dd
children.text: such other matters as the Committee considers appropriate.

children.heading: Updates
children.label: ii Updates
children.text: The Committee shall periodically reconsider and adjust the amount of the fee to be assessed under subparagraph (A) with respect to a covered transaction to ensure that the amount of the fee does not exceed the costs of administering this section and otherwise remains appropriate.

children.heading: Deposit and availability of fees
children.label: C Deposit and availability of fees
i
children.text: be deposited into the Fund solely for use in carrying out activities under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent and in the amounts provided in advance in appropriations Acts, be available to the chairperson;

children.label: iii
children.text: remain available until expended; and

children.label: iv
children.text: be in addition to any appropriations made available to the members of the Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , the chairperson, in consultation with the Committee, shall complete a study of the feasibility and merits of establishing a fee or fee scale to prioritize the timing of the response of the Committee to a draft or formal written notice during the period before the Committee accepts the formal written notice under subsection (b)(1)(C)(i), in the event that the Committee is unable to respond during the time required by subclause (II) of that subsection because of an unusually large influx of notices, or for other reasons.August 13, 2018

children.heading: Submission to Congress
children.label: ii Submission to Congress
children.text: After completing the study required by clause (i), the chairperson, or a designee of the chairperson, shall submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a report on the findings of the study.

children.heading: Transfer of funds
children.label: 4 Transfer of funds
children.text: To the extent provided in advance in appropriations Acts, the chairperson may transfer any amounts in the Fund to any other department or agency represented on the Committee for the purpose of addressing emerging needs in carrying out activities under this section. Amounts so transferred shall be in addition to any other amounts available to that department or agency for that purpose.

children.heading: Centralization of certain Committee functions
children.label: q Centralization of certain Committee functions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The chairperson, in consultation with the Committee, may centralize certain functions of the Committee within the Department of the Treasury for the purpose of enhancing interagency coordination and collaboration in carrying out the functions of the Committee under this section.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: 2 Functions
children.text: Functions that may be centralized under paragraph (1) include identifying non-notified and non-declared transactions pursuant to subsection (b)(1)(H), and other functions as determined by the chairperson and the Committee.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as limiting the authority of any department or agency represented on the Committee to represent its own interests before the Committee.
